title: Doctrine: session-local settings churn belongs in settings.local.json (D5)
action: Per CONTEXT D5 — prose only, no code. Add one short rule to the doctrine layer (docs/specs/doctrine-layer.md Business Rules, in the voice of the neighbouring rules) and a matching line wherever the repo tells a human how to configure a session (check skills/bee-hive/references/routing-and-contracts.md and AGENTS.block.md for the natural home; if none fits, doctrine-layer alone is enough — do NOT invent a new doc): machine- and session-local keys (env toggles, personal permissions) belong in the gitignored `.claude/settings.local.json`; the tracked `.claude/settings.json` carries only team-durable wiring (hooks, statusline). Cite D5 (decision `189f1e1f`) per citation discipline. Explain the WHY in one clause: a tracked settings file mutated by every session shows up as dirt in every concurrent session's `git status` — the observed complaint. Render + manifest. TARGETED verify: release_manifest --check.

truths: doctrine-layer carries the settings.local.json rule with its why, citing 189f1e1f (grep-visible)
no new doc file invented; the rule lands in an existing surface
prohibitions: Do not untrack .claude/settings.json itself — it carries team hook wiring

trace.dropped_reason: re-scoped: user clarified the real problem is forgotten scratch/report files with no canonical home + no sweep, not .bak backups
